title: sesleria autumnalis subsp. anatolica
intro: Sesleria autumnalis subsp. anatolica, also known as Anatolian autumn-grass or Anatolian autumn sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, characterized by its compact, clump-forming habit and delicate, blue-green foliage. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Sesleria autumnalis subsp. anatolica are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
